mnemonics: ACF Fades Gradually For Stationary Friends: A stationary process's Autocorrelation Function (ACF) fades gradually to zero, unlike a non-stationary one.
keyPoints: A stationary time series has a constant mean and variance over time.
The Autocorrelation Function (ACF) for a stationary process decays (damps) to zero relatively quickly.
A non-stationary series, like one with a trend, often has an ACF that decays very slowly.
commonMistakes: Assuming a high autocorrelation at lag 1 implies non-stationarity; it is the pattern of decay (slow vs. fast) that is more important.
Confusing stationarity with the absence of autocorrelation; a stationary series can still be highly autocorrelated.
